hero.title: Study ICT at ITSOEH.
hero.subtitle: Join Information and Communications Technology Engineering and learn to build real solutions with software, networks, data, artificial intelligence, and the Internet of Things.

admission.title: 2026–2027 admission process.
admission.subtitle: Check key dates for application, diagnostic evaluation, and results.

requirements.title: Application requirements.
requirements.subtitle: Prepare your documents in PDF format for registration.

study.title: Academic transcript or certificate
study.body: High school transcript, certificate, or signed academic record with a minimum GPA of 7.0.

payment.title: Payment receipt
payment.body: Application fee payment receipt for $651.00 MXN.

gmail.title: Gmail account
gmail.body: You must have a Gmail email address.

foreign.title: International students
foreign.body: Must complete the corresponding academic equivalency process.

why.title: Why study ICT?
why.subtitle: ITICs combines technical training, real projects, certifications, community, and high-growth career areas.

build.title: Learn by building
build.body: Develop projects in software, networks, databases, IoT, cybersecurity, and artificial intelligence.

certs.title: Official certifications
certs.body: Access official Huawei and Cisco academies to strengthen your technical profile.

community.title: Technical community
community.body: Join robotics, IoT, development, and entrepreneurship clubs.

portfolio.title: Real portfolio
portfolio.body: Document and publish projects on GitHub as evidence of your skills.

learning.title: What you can learn in the program.

programming.title: Programming
programming.body: Application development, logic, data structures, web, and mobile.

networks.title: Networks
networks.body: Design, configuration, and administration of computer and telecommunications networks.

databases.title: Databases
databases.body: Database design and administration for real systems.

ai.title: AI and data
ai.body: Data usage, emerging tools, and intelligent systems.

iot.title: IoT and hardware
iot.body: Sensors, microcontrollers, embedded systems, and connected devices.

security.title: Cybersecurity
security.body: Information protection, auditing, and network security management.

programming.question: Do I need to know programming before applying?
programming.answer: No. The program starts from fundamentals. What matters is your willingness to practice, solve problems, and learn consistently.

math.question: Is there a lot of math?
math.answer: Yes, because it is an engineering program, but you don't need to master math before entering. It is taught progressively and applied to programming, networks, data, communications, and decision-making.

mathFear.question: What if I'm not good at math?
mathFear.answer: You can improve. Many students enter with doubts or fear, but advance through practice, tutoring, teamwork, and consistency.

ai.question: Will AI replace engineers?
ai.answer: AI does not replace engineers who can think, design, integrate, and solve problems. It becomes a tool for those who know how to use it, build with it, and apply it correctly.

jobs.question: Are there job opportunities?
jobs.answer: Yes. You can grow in software, networks, databases, cybersecurity, IoT, support, infrastructure, data, cloud, automation, and technology project management.

laptop.question: Do I need a laptop?
laptop.answer: It is recommended to have a laptop for practicing programming, databases, networks, web design, and projects. It doesn't have to be expensive at first, but should allow you to work with development tools.

contact.title: Questions about admission?
contact.subtitle: Contact the program or the Registrar's Office for guidance on application, requirements, and admission process.

finalCta.title: Your path in ITICs can start today.
finalCta.subtitle: Review the admission notice, prepare your documents, and reach out to the ITICs community to resolve your questions before applying.
